evidence: The layering api->query->utils is respected and no obvious import cycles exist.

The `query` package has become a hub: it contains the BlackLab executor, the snippet parser, the semantic explorer, the predicate mini-framework (7 classes), the CQL verifier, result types, and token types — 16 classes with at least 4 distinct concerns.

WordSketchApiServer imports five inner result types directly from SemanticFieldExplorer (ComparisonResult, AdjectiveProfile, ExplorationResult, DiscoveredNoun, CoreAdjective), coupling the API layer to implementation internals of the query layer.
BlackLabQueryExecutor stores `indexPath` as a field but never reads it after the constructor — a dead field that signals copy-paste growth.
A leftover temp file `HybridQueryExecutor.java.tmp.16216.1771545912963` sits alongside production sources, and `blacklab-core-pom.xml` is an orphaned secondary POM in the project root.

CQLParser and CQLPattern (grammar package) are used only by CQLVerifier (query package), which itself is not on the main BlackLab execution path — a parallel CQL path whose role from an architecture perspective is opaque.
Most top-level packages (api, config, grammar, indexer, utils, viz, tagging) map to clear domain capabilities.

The `query` package is overloaded: 16 classes mixing BlackLab execution, snippet parsing, semantic exploration, predicate matching, token types, and result DTOs. A new engineer cannot predict what belongs here.
WordSketchApiServer is 1042 lines with all handler logic inlined as anonymous lambda bodies, blurring the boundary between routing and business logic and making ownership hard to describe.

CQLVerifier and its 7-class predicate framework (TokenPredicate, AbstractPatternPredicate, AndPredicate, OrPredicate, NotPredicate, LemmaPredicate, TagPredicate, PosGroupPredicate, WordPredicate) form a self-contained in-memory query engine inside the query package, but are disconnected from the active BlackLab execution path — their purpose is unclear without reading tests.
SimpleTagger exists in the tagging package with no PosTagger interface to implement against; CLAUDE.md lists tagging/PosTagger.java as an existing interface but the file does not exist.
QueryResults is a utility class whose only purpose is to namespace inner types WordSketchResult and ConcordanceResult — a structural accident, not a domain concept.

CQLParser declares inner subclasses AgreementRule and PatternElement that extend the corresponding CQLPattern inner classes with zero added behavior — their sole purpose is re-export convenience, blurring the grammar/query boundary.

QueryExecutor interface declares executeBcqlQuery, executeSurfacePattern, and isReady as default methods that either forward to another interface method or return a constant — these defaults weaken the contract and make it unclear what the interface actually requires.

AbstractPatternPredicate.test() calls wildcardToRegex() and Pattern.matches() on every invocation without caching the compiled Pattern — an abstraction that fails to deliver the performance its name implies.

CQLPattern.Constraint represents three mutually exclusive structural variants (simple, OR, AND) in one class, using orConstraints/andConstraints list emptiness as a discriminant — callers must consult isOr() and isAnd() to understand which shape they have rather than relying on types.
TokenPredicate.negate() is a default method returning a lambda, but NotPredicate is a separate explicit class doing the same thing — parallel ways to express negation without a stated reason.

delegation_density: BlackLabSnippetParser exposes ~15 public static methods including two public Pattern constants (LEMMA_ATTR, XPOS_ATTR, UPOS_ATTR) that callers (BlackLabQueryExecutor) access directly — the class is a low-cohesion static dump.

The postgresql JDBC driver (org.postgresql:postgresql:42.7.1) is declared as a runtime dependency but no JDBC or SQL code is present anywhere in the production source tree — unused dependency bloats the shaded jar.

Four source files are excluded from compilation in pom.xml (CQLToLuceneCompiler, HybridQueryExecutor, ConcordanceExplorer, CollocationWitnessTool) — they remain in the source tree but cannot be built, signalling an incomplete migration.
HybridQueryExecutor.java.tmp.16216.1771545912963 is a leftover temp file in the query source directory.
blacklab-core-pom.xml exists alongside the authoritative pom.xml in the project root with no reference from the build — orphaned artifact.
The two BlackLab dependencies (blacklab-engine and blacklab-query-parser) are clearly necessary and distinct.
fastjson2 (com.alibaba), slf4j, and JUnit 5 are lean and appropriate for the purpose; no obvious duplicate-library conflicts.

AbstractPatternPredicate.test() calls wildcardToRegex() then Pattern.matches() (which compiles a fresh Pattern object) on every invocation — no caching of the compiled regex despite the pattern string being immutable.

PatternSubstitution.substituteCollocate() contains a hand-rolled CQL tokenizer (the while-loop over i) that handles only '[...]' and '"..."' cases, silently skipping distance modifiers, numbered prefixes, and anything outside those two forms — fragile with no error signalling.
WordSketchApiServer inlines all handler logic as anonymous lambdas inside start(), producing deeply nested closures across 1042 lines; individual handler paths are hard to test or trace in isolation.

ConcordanceResult has 5 constructors, each null-filling a different subset of 11 fields — accretive growth in lieu of distinct result types; callers cannot know which fields are populated without reading all constructors.
BlackLabQueryExecutor.getCorpusSize() has a formatting artifact (body starts at column 0, misaligned with the rest of the class) and returns a hard-coded 0L stub — visible incomplete implementation.
RadialPlot.toSVG() mixes raw string concatenation (svg.append("<svg ...>" + width + ...)) with String.format() calls in the same method without a stated style preference.
GrammarConfigLoader.RelationConfig.substituteHeadword() is a 60-line static method embedded in a Java record — complex parsing logic residing in a type that is supposed to be a data carrier.

strengths: HttpApiUtils centralizes all HTTP response concerns (sendJsonResponse, sendError, parseQueryParams); every handler delegates uniformly without ad-hoc response construction.
QueryExecutor interface provides a clean, typed boundary between the API layer and query execution; IOException propagates predictably up to handlers.
PatternSubstitution isolates the collocate-substitution concern into one focused utility used from exactly one call site.
Error translation is consistent: IOException caught at each handler entry point and mapped to 500 via HttpApiUtils.sendError.
mid_level_elegance.issue_character: The seam issues are concentrated at the WordSketchApiServer–SemanticFieldExplorer boundary: multi-seed orchestration is inlined in the handler rather than delegated to the explorer, while the single-seed path delegates properly, creating an asymmetric and tangled boundary. Response mapping (result → Map) is repeated verbatim across several handlers with no shared translation layer.

strengths: Most comments in BlackLabQueryExecutor explain non-obvious decisions rather than restating code (e.g. why HitPropertyHitText returns plain text, why the collocate is extracted as the last whitespace token).
Small predicate classes (LemmaPredicate, TagPredicate, AndPredicate, OrPredicate, NotPredicate) are admirably lean — no docstring bloat on trivial one-method classes.
ai_generated_debt.issue_character: Debt is concentrated in two patterns: (1) noisy debug logging throughout PatternSubstitution.substituteCollocate that traces every intermediate variable at every step, and (2) Javadoc on QueryExecutor interface methods that restates method-signature parameters without adding domain insight.

strengths: No deprecated symbols called by active code; no mixed-language files for the same module.
migration_signals is empty — no mechanical markers of stale migration work detected.
Grammar config primary path (pattern key) is clear and consistently used in tests and production.
incomplete_migration.issue_character: Low-level residue with no tracked removal: a cql_pattern fallback in GrammarConfigLoader, an old %s-placeholder detection in findCollocations, and a normalizeRelationId shim in WordSketchApiServer mapping obsolete relation ID strings to current ones. None are annotated with TODO/FIXME for removal.

strengths: api/, grammar/, config/, utils/, viz/, and indexer/blacklab/ packages each have cohesive, single-concern populations.
utils/ correctly holds generic shared utilities (LogDiceCalculator, LongIntHashMap) imported across multiple packages.
grammar/ cohesively groups CQL parsing: CQLParser, CQLPattern, CQLParseException — no straggler concerns.
package_organization.issue_character: The query/ package (17 files) is overloaded with at least four distinct concern clusters: predicate DSL (Token, TokenWindow, *Predicate classes), query execution (QueryExecutor, BlackLab*), semantic exploration (SemanticFieldExplorer), and result types (QueryResults). CQLVerifier imports heavily from grammar/ but lives in query/, an import-affinity mismatch.

strengths: WordSketchApiServer constructor stores state only; start() is a separate explicit call — construction has no implicit side effects.
No static mutable fields or static initializer blocks anywhere in production code.
BlackLabQueryExecutor constructor declares throws IOException explicitly, making its file-I/O dependency visible to callers.
No environment variable reads at class load time.
initialization_coupling.issue_character: The only notable concern is GrammarConfigLoader.createDefaultEnglish() relying on a CWD-relative path; if called as a static field initializer by a consumer it would be fragile. In practice Main.java calls it dynamically. AbstractPatternPredicate compiles its regex on every test() call rather than caching at construction — a performance concern adjacent to initialization, not a true coupling issue.

evidence: WordSketchApiServer.java is 1042 lines with 11+ handler methods, each containing inline parameter parsing AND manual JSONObject response construction — routing, parsing, and serialization are not separated.

Five or more handlers (handleFullSketch, handleRelationQuery, handleFullDependencySketch, handleDependencyRelationQuery, handleSemanticField) contain structurally identical collocation-list building code: loop over results, create JSONObject, put lemma/frequency/logDice/pos — copy-pasted without extraction to a helper.

handleSemanticFieldExploreMulti (lines 648-718) embeds the collocate-intersection algorithm inline: accumulates seedToCollocates, computes commonCollocates via retainAll — business logic that belongs in SemanticFieldExplorer.

SemanticFieldExplorer.java (690 lines) houses five result data classes (ExplorationResult, DiscoveredNoun, CoreAdjective, ComparisonResult, AdjectiveProfile, Edge) that logically belong alongside QueryResults.WordSketchResult/ConcordanceResult in QueryResults.java.

GrammarConfigLoader.RelationConfig record has grown to 8+ methods (getFullPattern, substituteHeadword, mergeLemmaConstraint, extractXposPattern, extractPosPattern, extractConstraintAttribute, collocatePosGroup, getDeprel) — a data record has become a service object with pattern-manipulation responsibilities.

BlackLabQueryExecutor contains extractDependentLemmaFromGroup and extractPosFromGroup (lines 404-446) that duplicate BlackLabSnippetParser.LEMMA_ATTR/XPOS_ATTR parsing logic already available on the parser class.

BlackLabSnippetParser.extractCollocateFromPattern (lines 164-170) is documented as 'Extract collocate lemma from the BCQL pattern' but always returns the string literal 'unknown' — the computed pos2Index value is never used.
BlackLabQueryExecutor.getCorpusSize (lines 532-534) returns 0L always with no documentation of stub/not-implemented status, silently breaking any caller expecting corpus-size data.

QueryResults.ConcordanceResult has 5 constructors that produce objects with different combinations of null fields (lemma, tag, word, rawXml, collocateLemma). The type cannot distinguish a 'full concordance result' from a 'grouped collocate result'; callers using getLemma() on a grouped result always receive null.

CQLVerifier.verify and CQLVerifier.verifyForCollocate (lines 77-118) have identical implementations — same buildSteps call, same matchSequence call, same return — suggesting they honour a fictional contract difference.

GrammarConfigLoader.createDefaultEnglish wraps IOException in an unchecked IllegalStateException without documenting this conversion, silently changing the checked/unchecked contract visible to callers.
BlackLabSnippetParser.extractCollocateFromPattern computes pos2Index via findLabelPosition then discards the result and returns 'unknown' unconditionally — dead code in the branch.

CQLVerifier.matchSequence (lines 224-253): window.findInRange is called identically in every iteration of the 'rep' loop (for rep = step.minRep to step.maxRep) even though the candidate list is independent of rep — redundant repeated work.
SimpleTagger.determineVerbForm returns 'VBD' (past tense) for the word 'am' (line 354), but 'am' is present tense and should map to 'VBP' — a logic error in the tagger fallback.

handleSemanticFieldExploreMulti initialises commonCollocates to null, then at line 666-668 has a null-guard 'if (commonCollocates == null) commonCollocates = new HashSet<>()' that can only trigger when seeds is empty — but the seeds.size() < 2 guard at line 611 already exits before the loop for empty seeds, making the null-guard unreachable.
handleBcqlQueryPost declares 'boolean raw = obj.getBooleanValue("raw")' (line 984) but the variable is never read in the method body — dead variable whose presence misleads the reader.
relationType in GrammarConfigLoader.RelationConfig is a plain String (nullable), yet every handler filters by the literals 'SURFACE' and 'DEP' — no enum means typos are invisible to the compiler.
RelationConfig.explorationEnabled is declared as boxed Boolean (nullable), not boolean. The value's absence is not documented; callers must null-check but nothing in the record forces them to do so.

QueryResults.ConcordanceResult exposes getLemma(), getTag(), getWord(), getRawXml(), getCollocateLemma() — all of which may return null depending on which constructor was used. The Java type system cannot prevent callers from using the wrong getter on the wrong constructor's result.

POS group values ('adj', 'verb', 'noun', 'adv', 'prep', 'other') are scattered as raw string literals across SemanticFieldExplorer, GrammarConfigLoader, and SimpleTagger with no enum or constant to tie them together.

relation type sentinel 'unknown' returned from BlackLabSnippetParser methods (e.g., extractPosFromMatch returns 'unknown', extractCollocateFromPattern returns 'unknown') mixes a sentinel string into String return types without a wrapper type.

SemanticFieldExplorer result data classes — AdjectiveProfile, CoreAdjective, DiscoveredNoun — and their fields (seedAdjectives, sharedAdjectives, coreAdjectives) hard-code 'Adjective/Noun' even though the explorer is now generic and driven by any BCQL relation (could discover verb collocates, etc.).
BlackLabSnippetParser.extractCollocateFromPattern: name says 'extract' but the method always returns 'unknown' — name/behavior mismatch.

WordSketchApiServer.normalizeRelationId: 'normalize' is vague; the method is an alias resolver mapping user-facing strings ('adj_predicate', 'modifier') to internal relation IDs. Better name: resolveRelationAlias or mapRelationAlias.
CQLVerifier.verify and CQLVerifier.verifyForCollocate have different names suggesting different contracts, but their implementations are identical.

BlackLabSnippetParser is a name suggesting XML snippet parsing, but the class also handles plain-text extraction (extractPlainTextFromXml, detokenize), BCQL pattern inspection (findLabelPosition, extractHeadword), and collocate extraction from plain text — the name under-describes the class scope.
Duplicate Javadoc comment in WordSketchApiServer at line 903-904: 'Render radial plot.' repeated twice on consecutive lines.
api/ package mixes strategies heavily: 17 try-catch blocks alongside 3 returns_null and 1 throw, with no consistent policy
query/ package uses all four strategies simultaneously: 16 returns_null, 9 try-catch, 8 throws, 6 result_type — no dominant approach
getTotalFrequency() in BlackLabQueryExecutor silently swallows all exceptions (catch(Exception e) { return 0L; }) without logging, hiding index access failures
HttpApiUtils.parseQueryParams() silently skips invalid URL parameters (catch block is a comment-only no-op: 'Skip invalid parameters'), losing decode errors
BlackLabQueryExecutor constructor wraps and rethrows with context (throw new IOException('...', e)) — good pattern — but is not used consistently elsewhere
handleFullSketch() catches Exception broadly and only logs at DEBUG level (logger.debug()), not WARN/ERROR, so relation failures are invisible in production

Integer.parseInt() / Double.parseDouble() called bare (no try-catch) on raw query parameters in handleSemanticFieldExplore, handleSemanticFieldExploreMulti, handleConcordanceExamples — a NumberFormatException from a bad request produces an unhandled 500 instead of a 400

config/ uses throws + returns_null (7 cases) for field-not-found cases; query/ uses Optional returns for some lookups (grammarConfig.getRelation()) but null checks for others — inconsistent boundary contract

BlackLabSnippetParser fields (LEMMA_ATTR, XPOS_ATTR, etc.) are package-private (no modifier) instead of private — breaks the expected Java convention of private static final for internal constants; other classes use private for similar patterns

WordSketchApiServer uses fully-qualified type com.sun.net.httpserver.HttpServer and com.sun.net.httpserver.HttpExchange inline in method signatures instead of importing them at the top of the file — inconsistent with the rest of the codebase's import style

BlackLabQueryExecutor has two public methods (findDependencyCollocations, executeDependencyPattern) that are NOT in the QueryExecutor interface — they form a wider public API that only callers using the concrete type can access, violating the interface-based abstraction pattern used everywhere else

SemanticFieldExplorer has two constructors: one taking indexPath (String) that creates its own BlackLabQueryExecutor, and one taking QueryExecutor for injection — the first constructor is a factory-style outlier vs. the dependency-injection pattern used in WordSketchApiServer and tests
handleVisualRadial() doc comment contains 'Render radial plot.' duplicated twice in the Javadoc — copy-paste artifact not present in sibling handlers

Regex patterns in BlackLabSnippetParser use Java-standard java.util.regex.Pattern.compile() but are declared as package-private fields rather than private, creating an inconsistency with all other utility classes in utils/ that keep internals private
WordSketchApiServer (1042 LOC, 10+ endpoint handlers) has zero dedicated tests — the most complex and user-facing class in the codebase is entirely untested
SemanticFieldExplorer (core business logic for the /explore and /explore-multi endpoints) has no unit tests whatsoever, despite having a testable constructor that accepts a mock QueryExecutor

All BlackLab integration tests (BlackLabBcqlConcordanceTest, BlackLabDummyTest, DependencySketchTest) are guarded by assumeTrue on a hardcoded Windows path ('D:\corpora_philsci\bi') that does not exist in CI — they effectively never run in automated builds

GrammarConfigLoader is tested only through HybridAlgorithmTest which calls createDefaultEnglish() — there are no tests for the constructor that takes a Path (the production codepath), missing coverage for invalid configs, missing version, copulas key present, empty relations array
CQLVerifierTest provides meaningful unit coverage for the CQLVerifier but does NOT test negative/edge cases: empty pattern, null window, patterns with no matching tokens
No cross-module integration test exists that exercises the full request→query→score→response pipeline even with a mock index

Integer.parseInt() / Double.parseDouble() calls on untrusted input in API handlers have no tests for malformed input (e.g., top=abc), meaning the 500-instead-of-400 bug is not caught by the test suite

LogDiceCalculatorTest (241 lines) and LongIntHashMapTest (80 lines) show good unit test quality for pure utility classes — the test strategy works well when applied, but is not applied to the higher-risk business logic

QueryExecutor interface omits findDependencyCollocations and executeDependencyPattern — these are public methods on BlackLabQueryExecutor only, leaking implementation-specific surface outside the abstraction

The /api/sketch/{lemma} endpoint returns a top-level 'patterns' key containing relation data, but /api/sketch/{lemma}/{relation} returns a top-level 'collocations' key — sibling endpoints with different shapes for what is conceptually the same resource

handleFullDependencySketch returns a 'relations' key, handleRelationQuery returns a 'collocations' key, and handleDependencyRelationQuery also returns a 'collocations' key — three response shapes for analogous operations with no documented contract

Numeric query parameters (top, min_logdice, min_shared, nouns_per, limit, max) are parsed with Integer.parseInt/Double.parseDouble without validation or error messages — the API does not communicate what valid ranges are and silently crashes on bad input

handleSemanticFieldExploreMulti uses bcqlPattern = relationConfig.get().pattern() (the raw pattern) while handleSemanticFieldExplore uses bcqlPattern = relationConfig.get().getFullPattern(seed) (with head substituted) — inconsistent treatment of the same config object in sibling handlers
POST endpoints (/api/bcql, /api/visual/radial) explicitly validate the HTTP method and return 405, but GET endpoints do not — a POST to /api/sketch/{lemma} silently processes the request

executeSurfacePattern is a default method on QueryExecutor that falls back to findCollocations, but BlackLabQueryExecutor overrides it with a different algorithm — callers using the QueryExecutor interface get different behavior than those using BlackLabQueryExecutor directly, without documentation
The application is a single-user local research tool with no multi-user or network-facing deployment requirement documented — absence of authentication is intentional by design
All endpoints serve read-only corpus query results; there are no write operations, user data, or sensitive resources exposed
CORS is set to Access-Control-Allow-Origin: * on all responses — appropriate for a local tool, but would be a finding if this were a shared/production deployment
No user input reaches file-system paths directly — index path is set at server startup from CLI args, not from request parameters
The tool operates entirely on a pre-built read-only Lucene index; there are no admin endpoints, no configuration mutation endpoints, and no authentication bypass paths

issues.summary: query package is a hub mixing 4+ distinct concerns across 16 classes
evidence: The package contains: BlackLab execution (BlackLabQueryExecutor), snippet parsing (BlackLabSnippetParser), semantic exploration (SemanticFieldExplorer), a 7-class predicate mini-framework (TokenPredicate through WordPredicate), CQL in-memory verification (CQLVerifier), result DTOs (QueryResults), and token value types (Token, TokenWindow) — 16 files, 4+ concerns.

The predicate framework and CQLVerifier are disconnected from the active BlackLab execution path; they form a self-contained parallel query evaluator whose blast radius would be wide if the package is refactored.
issues.suggestion: Extract sub-packages: query/execution (BlackLabQueryExecutor, QueryExecutor, QueryResults), query/verify (CQLVerifier, TokenPredicate, predicate classes, Token, TokenWindow), query/exploration (SemanticFieldExplorer). Move BlackLabSnippetParser into query/execution as a package-private helper. This narrows change blast radius and makes each sub-package's purpose self-evident.

issues.summary: WordSketchApiServer imports 5 inner result types directly from SemanticFieldExplorer, coupling API to query implementation internals
evidence: WordSketchApiServer.java lines 13-17 import SemanticFieldExplorer.ComparisonResult, SemanticFieldExplorer.AdjectiveProfile, SemanticFieldExplorer.ExplorationResult, SemanticFieldExplorer.DiscoveredNoun, SemanticFieldExplorer.CoreAdjective.
These are inner classes of an implementation class, not an interface or DTO package — any refactor of SemanticFieldExplorer requires editing the API layer.
issues.suggestion: Move the five result types to a dedicated query/results or query/exploration package as top-level classes. Reference them by their own types in both SemanticFieldExplorer and WordSketchApiServer. This breaks the direct import dependency on the implementation class.

issues.summary: BlackLabQueryExecutor stores indexPath field but never reads it after construction
evidence: Line 54: `private final String indexPath;` — field is assigned in constructor and passed to BlackLab.open(), but the field itself is never accessed after construction.
Storing a value only for a one-time side-effect suggests accretive growth (possibly needed by a previous implementation).
issues.suggestion: Remove the indexPath field. Use the constructor parameter directly in the BlackLab.open() call and discard it afterwards.

issues.summary: Temp file and secondary POM left in source tree alongside production files
evidence: HybridQueryExecutor.java.tmp.16216.1771545912963 is a raw editor temp file with a PID/timestamp suffix sitting in the query source directory.
blacklab-core-pom.xml exists in the project root but is not referenced by the main pom.xml or any build script.
issues.suggestion: Delete both files. Add *.tmp and *.tmp.* patterns to .gitignore to prevent future temp file commits.

issues.summary: WordSketchApiServer is a 1042-line god class mixing routing, parameter parsing, and business logic for 10+ endpoints
evidence: The file is 1042 lines. All handler logic lives as anonymous lambda bodies inside start(), creating deep nesting and preventing independent testability of individual endpoints.

The class handles: health check, full word sketch, per-relation sketch, dependency sketch, relations list, semantic field exploration, comparison, radial plot SVG, concordance, and grammar config endpoints — ownership spans the entire API surface.
issues.suggestion: Extract each endpoint group into a dedicated handler class (e.g., SketchHandler, SemanticFieldHandler, ConcordanceHandler) following a HandlerContext pattern that receives QueryExecutor and GrammarConfigLoader. WordSketchApiServer becomes a thin router that registers them. Each handler class is independently testable.

issues.summary: 7-class in-memory predicate framework (CQLVerifier + predicates) lives in query package but is disconnected from the active execution path
evidence: BlackLabQueryExecutor never imports or references CQLVerifier, TokenPredicate, or any predicate class — the main execution path goes entirely through BlackLab's own parser.

CQLVerifier imports CQLPattern from the grammar package and constructs predicates, but nothing in the production path calls verify() outside of tests. This creates a parallel in-memory query evaluation subsystem whose role is invisible to a reader of the production code.
issues.suggestion: Either (a) document explicitly that CQLVerifier is a test-only utility and move it to src/test, or (b) integrate it into the production path where it provides value (e.g., pre-flight validation before sending to BlackLab). Move the predicate classes to the same location so the framework's scope is clear.

issues.summary: SimpleTagger has no PosTagger interface despite CLAUDE.md documenting one as existing
evidence: CLAUDE.md lists `tagging/PosTagger.java` as a `PosTagger interface` in the code structure table.
No PosTagger.java file exists anywhere in the source tree. SimpleTagger declares tagSentence, tagSentences, getName, getTagset methods but implements no named interface.
Without the interface, SimpleTagger cannot be substituted or mocked — the documentation contract drifts from the runtime reality.
issues.suggestion: Create PosTagger.java in the tagging package declaring tagSentence, tagSentences, getName, getTagset. Have SimpleTagger implement it. Update CLAUDE.md to reflect the actual state until this is done, or create the interface immediately.

issues.summary: QueryResults utility class exists only to namespace inner types, not as a domain concept
evidence: QueryResults has a private constructor and two inner static classes: WordSketchResult and ConcordanceResult. The class itself adds no behavior — it is a Java package-equivalent using a class as a namespace.
Callers use QueryResults.WordSketchResult and QueryResults.ConcordanceResult — the outer class name adds noise without communicating domain meaning.
issues.suggestion: Promote WordSketchResult and ConcordanceResult to top-level classes in the query package (or a query/results subpackage). Delete the QueryResults wrapper. This removes false indirection and makes imports cleaner.

issues.summary: CQLParser.AgreementRule and CQLParser.PatternElement subclass their CQLPattern counterparts with zero added behavior
evidence: CQLParser.AgreementRule extends CQLPattern.AgreementRule with only a constructor that calls super() — no fields, no methods, no overrides.
CQLParser.PatternElement extends CQLPattern.PatternElement with only two constructors that call super() — no fields, no methods, no overrides.
These exist solely so that callers can write CQLParser.AgreementRule instead of CQLPattern.AgreementRule, which blurs which package owns these types.
issues.suggestion: Remove CQLParser.AgreementRule and CQLParser.PatternElement. Callers should reference CQLPattern.AgreementRule and CQLPattern.PatternElement directly. Update ParsedCQL to use CQLPattern.PatternElement in its list.

issues.summary: QueryExecutor interface has 3 default methods that fall back to other methods, weakening what the interface actually requires
evidence: executeBcqlQuery default: `return executeQuery(bcqlPattern, maxResults)` — the comment says 'backward compatibility'.
executeSurfacePattern default: `return findCollocations(...)` — ignores position hints entirely.
isReady default: `return true` — silently succeeds for all implementations.
With 3 of 7 non-close methods having defaults, an implementor can satisfy the interface by implementing only 3 methods while the others silently fall back, making the contract untrustworthy.
issues.suggestion: Remove the default implementations for executeBcqlQuery and executeSurfacePattern. Promote them to abstract methods or separate them into a richer interface (e.g., SurfacePatternExecutor extends QueryExecutor). Keep isReady as a default only if all callers truly treat false as optional.

issues.summary: CQLPattern.Constraint encodes three mutually exclusive structural variants in one class using list-emptiness as discriminant
evidence: CQLPattern.Constraint has both orConstraints and andConstraints lists plus isOr() and isAnd() boolean methods.
Three variants: simple (both lists empty), OR (orConstraints non-empty), AND (andConstraints non-empty). Callers must query both predicates to determine which variant they hold.
The and() factory method returns different types depending on constraints.length — either the single element or a new Constraint with andConstraints populated, conflating factory with constructor.
issues.suggestion: Model the three variants as a sealed hierarchy: SimpleConstraint(field, pattern, negated), OrConstraint(List<SimpleConstraint>), AndConstraint(List<SimpleConstraint>). Use a sealed interface or sealed abstract class Constraint. This makes variant dispatch type-safe and eliminates the isOr/isAnd predicates.

issues.summary: AbstractPatternPredicate compiles a new Pattern on every test() call without caching
evidence: test() calls wildcardToRegex(effectivePattern) and Pattern.matches(regex, value) on every invocation. Pattern.matches() compiles a new Pattern object internally each time.
The pattern string is immutable (set once in constructor), so the compiled Pattern could be cached as a final field.
issues.suggestion: Compute the compiled Pattern in the constructor and store it as a final field: `private final Pattern compiled = Pattern.compile(wildcardToRegex(...))`. Replace the Pattern.matches() call with `compiled.matcher(value).matches()`. This makes test() O(1) in regex compilation cost.

issues.summary: PostgreSQL JDBC driver declared as runtime dependency but no database code exists in production sources
evidence: pom.xml declares `org.postgresql:postgresql:42.7.1` with `<scope>runtime</scope>`.
Grep of all .java files in src/main finds no import of java.sql, javax.sql, or org.postgresql — no JDBC code is present.
The dependency adds ~1MB to the shaded jar with no functional benefit.
issues.suggestion: Remove the postgresql dependency from pom.xml. If database support is planned, add it at the time it is needed and document the intent.

issues.summary: Four source files are excluded from compilation in pom.xml, leaving dead uncompilable code in the source tree
evidence: pom.xml maven-compiler-plugin excludes: CQLToLuceneCompiler.java, HybridQueryExecutor.java, ConcordanceExplorer.java, CollocationWitnessTool.java — described as 'Legacy Lucene 10 code not compatible with BlackLab Lucene 8.11.1'.
These files exist in the source tree but cannot be built, tested, or refactored — they create confusion about what is live code.
HybridQueryExecutor.java.tmp.* is an additional uncompilable artifact in the same directory.
issues.suggestion: Move excluded files to a clearly marked `src/main/java-legacy/` directory or a git branch, and delete the .tmp file. If the migration to BlackLab is complete, remove them entirely. Update the exclusions list in pom.xml accordingly.

issues.summary: AbstractPatternPredicate.test() recompiles wildcard-to-regex and Pattern on every invocation
evidence: test(): `String regex = wildcardToRegex(effectivePattern); return Pattern.matches(regex, value.toLowerCase());`
wildcardToRegex iterates the pattern string; Pattern.matches() compiles a new Pattern object. Both are called on every token test, even though the pattern is fixed at construction time.
issues.suggestion: Cache the compiled Pattern as a final field in the constructor. Use `compiled.matcher(value.toLowerCase()).matches()` in test(). This makes each test() call O(1) instead of O(pattern.length).

issues.summary: PatternSubstitution.substituteCollocate() uses a fragile hand-rolled CQL tokenizer that silently drops unrecognised syntax
evidence: The while-loop over `i` only recognises `[...]` and `"..."` tokens. Everything else increments i one character at a time without adding a position, silently ignoring distance modifiers ({0,3}), numbered prefixes (1:), and other CQL syntax.
If the pattern has a distance modifier between positions, the position count will be wrong and the substitution will target the wrong token silently.
issues.suggestion: Reuse the CQLParser from the grammar package to tokenise the pattern instead of re-implementing tokenisation. PatternSubstitution should call CQLParser.parseFull() and reconstruct the pattern from the parsed elements, substituting at the correct element index. Alternatively, add an explicit failure path when unrecognised syntax is encountered.

issues.summary: ConcordanceResult has 5 constructors null-filling different field subsets, signalling accretive growth rather than intentional design
evidence: ConcordanceResult has constructors with 7, 7 (grouped), 8 (with rawXml), 6 (no docId), and 4 (snippet-only) parameters.
Each constructor null-fills a different subset of the 11 fields (sentence, rawXml, lemma, tag, word, startOffset, endOffset, docId, collocateLemma, frequency, logDice).
Callers cannot determine which fields are populated without reading all 5 constructors. At least two structural variants exist: a per-hit concordance result and a grouped collocate result.
issues.suggestion: Separate into two distinct classes: ConcordanceResult (sentence context per hit: sentence, rawXml, start, end, docId) and CollocateResult (collocate-grouped: collocateLemma, frequency, logDice, examples). Each has one canonical constructor. This eliminates null-filled fields and makes each type's contract self-evident.

issues.summary: BlackLabQueryExecutor.getCorpusSize() is a stub returning 0L with a formatting artifact (wrong indentation)
evidence: The method body starts at column 0 unlike all other methods in the class, indicating a copy-paste or accidental edit.
The method returns 0L unconditionally — a stub that silently lies to any caller asking for the corpus size.
issues.suggestion: Either implement getCorpusSize() using blackLabIndex.tokens() / blackLabIndex.docs() (whichever BlackLab 4 exposes), or throw UnsupportedOperationException to fail loudly if called. Fix the indentation either way.

issues.summary: RadialPlot.toSVG() mixes raw string concatenation and String.format() in the same method without a consistent style
evidence: `svg.append("<svg xmlns=\"http://www.w3.org/2000/svg\" width=\"" + width + "\" height=\"" + height + ...)` — raw concatenation.
`svg.append(String.format("    <circle class=\"guide-circle\" cx=\"%s\" cy=\"%s\" r=\"%s\"/>\n", fmt(cx), ...))` — String.format.
The inconsistency makes the method harder to scan and edit safely.
issues.suggestion: Standardise on String.format() (or a text block if Java 15+) throughout toSVG(). Replace all concatenation-based appends with format calls that use named variables for dimensions.

issues.summary: GrammarConfigLoader.RelationConfig record contains a 60-line static substituteHeadword() parsing method inside a data type
evidence: RelationConfig is a Java record (a data carrier) but contains substituteHeadword() — a 60-line static method with its own inner loop that tokenises CQL patterns by scanning characters.
The method re-implements CQL position extraction logic that already exists in CQLParser and PatternSubstitution.
Records are idiomatic for immutable data carriers; embedding complex parsing logic inside one violates the extraction level balance the dimension looks for.
issues.suggestion: Extract substituteHeadword() to PatternSubstitution (or a dedicated PatternHeadwordSubstitution class) that reuses CQLParser for tokenisation. RelationConfig.getFullPattern() can delegate to the extracted helper. This makes RelationConfig a pure data record and centralises CQL pattern manipulation.

issues.summary: Multi-seed exploration logic inlined in handler instead of delegated to SemanticFieldExplorer, asymmetric with single-seed path
evidence: handleSemanticFieldExploreMulti builds seedToCollocates, computes commonCollocates intersection, and builds edges entirely inline in the handler (lines ~590-700 of WordSketchApiServer.java) rather than via SemanticFieldExplorer.
handleSemanticFieldExplore delegates entirely to explorer.exploreByPattern(...) and then formats the result; the two handlers have fundamentally different seam contracts.
SemanticFieldExplorer has no exploreMulti(...) method, so the multi-seed logic has no proper home and must be in the server.
issues.suggestion: Add an exploreMulti(Set<String> seeds, ...) method to SemanticFieldExplorer that returns a multi-seed ExplorationResult (or a new MultiExplorationResult). Move the intersection computation, edge building, and aggregation there. The handler should become: call exploreMulti, then format the result — mirroring the single-seed path.

issues.summary: Result-to-response translation (ExplorationResult → Map) repeated verbatim in multiple handlers without a shared mapper
evidence: handleSemanticFieldExplore manually builds seedCollocs, nouns, coreCollocs, and edges lists by iterating result fields with inline Math.round(...) calls.
handleSemanticField does the same Map-building loop for adjectives from ComparisonResult.
Both handlers share the same structural pattern: iterate result list, create Map<String,Object>, put fields with rounding, add to list.
issues.suggestion: Extract a ResponseMapper or ExplorationResultMapper class (in api/) with static methods like toSeedCollocatesJson(ExplorationResult), toEdgesJson(List<Edge>), etc. Each handler composes these methods. This removes the repetition and makes the translation boundary explicit.

issues.summary: SemanticFieldExplorer created and immediately closed inline in three handler methods, bypassing proper lifecycle management
evidence: handleSemanticFieldExplore: SemanticFieldExplorer explorer = new SemanticFieldExplorer(this.executor); ... explorer.close();
handleSemanticField: same pattern — new SemanticFieldExplorer(this.executor); ... explorer.close();
handleSemanticFieldExamples: same pattern again.
WordSketchApiServer already holds this.executor as a field; creating per-request wrappers around it that are immediately discarded is noise at the seam.
issues.suggestion: Since SemanticFieldExplorer(QueryExecutor) constructor stores the executor without owning it, it holds no exclusive resources. Create a single SemanticFieldExplorer as a field of WordSketchApiServer, constructed in the constructor alongside this.executor. Remove per-request construction and close() calls from all three handlers.

issues.summary: BlackLabQueryExecutor has three near-identical query dispatch paths with duplicated pattern-build, parse, group, extract-lemma boilerplate
evidence: findCollocations, findDependencyCollocations, and executeDependencyPattern each independently build a bcql string, call CorpusQueryLanguageParser.parse, call toQuery, call search().find(), call groupWithStoredHits, iterate groups, extract lemma, call buildAndRankCollocates.

Lemma extraction is done differently in each: findCollocations calls BlackLabSnippetParser.extractLemmaFromMatch then falls back to lastIndexOf(' '); executeDependencyPattern calls extractDependentLemmaFromGroup; findDependencyCollocations extracts identity directly.
The methods differ only in how they build the bcql pattern string, making the repeated dispatch boilerplate the dominant signal.
issues.suggestion: Extract a private executeGroupedQuery(String bcql, MatchSensitivity sensitivity, BiFunction<HitGroup, ?, String> lemmaExtractor) helper that handles the parse→query→find→group→iterate flow. Each public method builds its bcql string and lemma extractor, then delegates. This collapses three near-identical methods into focused entry points over a shared core.

issues.summary: PatternSubstitution.substituteCollocate has 6 debug log statements tracing every intermediate variable at each step
evidence: logger.debug("substituteCollocate: patternPositions.size()={}, collocatePosition={}", ...)
logger.debug("Pattern positions: {}", patternPositions)
logger.debug("Returning early - position > size")
logger.debug("originalConstraint at position {}: {}", collocatePosition, originalConstraint)
logger.debug("xposPattern: {}", xposPattern)
logger.debug("newConstraint: {}", newConstraint)
logger.debug("final result: {}", result)
Seven debug statements for a single 30-line method — every local variable is logged.
issues.suggestion: Remove all debug log statements from substituteCollocate. The method's logic is straightforward and self-documenting. If tracing is needed during development, use a single logger.debug at entry and one at exit with the input and output values.

issues.summary: QueryExecutor interface has verbose parameter-restating Javadoc on every method that adds no domain insight
evidence: @param headword The headword/lemma to find collocations for — restates the parameter name.
@param cqlPattern CQL pattern defining the collocate constraints — restates the type annotation.
@return List of collocation results, sorted by logDice descending — fully captured by return type and method name findCollocations.
executeSurfacePattern has a 7-line Javadoc block that is almost entirely parameter restatements.
getExecutorType: @return Executor type name (e.g., 'legacy', 'hybrid') on a method whose name is self-describing.
issues.suggestion: Strip Javadoc down to non-obvious contract details only. For findCollocations, keep only: what 'minLogDice=0' means, and that IOException signals index access failure. Remove all @param entries that simply restate names. Keep @return only when the return contract is non-obvious from the method signature.

issues.summary: WordSketchApiServer has several inline comments that restate the code rather than adding context
evidence: // Query each relation from grammar config — placed immediately before the loop that does exactly that.
// Add raw output option — on boolean raw = obj.getBooleanValue("raw");
// Health check endpoint — on server.createContext("/health", ...).
// Get word sketch for a lemma — on server.createContext("/api/sketch/", ...).
// Check if this is a dependency sketch request — immediately before if (parts.length > 1 && "dep".equals(parts[1])) { ... }.
issues.suggestion: Remove comments that literally restate the next line or block of code. Keep only comments that explain why a design decision was made, reference an external constraint, or warn about a non-obvious edge case.

issues.summary: GrammarConfigLoader silently accepts obsolete cql_pattern key alongside current pattern key with no removal tracking
evidence: String pattern = relObj.containsKey("pattern") ? relObj.getString("pattern") : (relObj.containsKey("cql_pattern") ? relObj.getString("cql_pattern") : "");
No comment, TODO, or @deprecated marker indicates when cql_pattern support will be removed.
The primary documentation (class Javadoc) shows only pattern, not cql_pattern, in the expected JSON structure.
issues.suggestion: Either (a) emit a deprecation warning via logger.warn when cql_pattern is encountered: 'Relation X uses deprecated cql_pattern key; rename to pattern', and add a TODO with a target version; or (b) if no external consumers use cql_pattern, remove the fallback entirely and update any existing config files.

issues.summary: normalizeRelationId in WordSketchApiServer is an untracked compatibility shim mapping old relation ID strings to current ones
evidence: private static String normalizeRelationId(String relation) { return switch (relation) { case "adj_modifier", "modifier" -> "noun_modifiers"; case "adj_predicate", "predicate" -> "noun_adj_predicates"; ... } }
No TODO, comment, or documentation explains when the old names (adj_modifier, adj_predicate, subject_of, object_of) will be removed or what clients still use them.
The method is called in handleSemanticFieldExplore and handleSemanticFieldExploreMulti — active code paths.
issues.suggestion: If external clients (web UI, documentation) still use the old names, document that explicitly with a comment referencing where clients are updated. If no external consumers depend on these aliases, remove the mapping and update callers. At minimum, add a TODO with a target release for removal.

issues.summary: findCollocations in BlackLabQueryExecutor contains an old %s-placeholder code path mixed with the current bracket-pattern style
evidence: if (cqlPattern.contains("%s")) { bcql = String.format(cqlPattern, headword.toLowerCase()); }
else if (cqlPattern.startsWith("[")) { bcql = String.format("\"%s\" %s", headword.toLowerCase(), cqlPattern); }
else { bcql = String.format("\"%s\" []", headword.toLowerCase()); }
Three pattern styles handled, suggesting this was expanded incrementally without retiring the old style.
issues.suggestion: Audit all callers of findCollocations to determine which pattern styles are actually in use. If %s-placeholder patterns are no longer passed by any caller, remove that branch. Document the expected pattern format in the method signature to prevent new callers from using the old style.

issues.summary: query/ package (17 files) is overloaded with four distinct concern clusters: predicate DSL, query execution, semantic exploration, and result types
evidence: query/ contains 17 files (from directory_profiles) — 10+ files with mixed concerns is a flat-directory overload threshold.

Cluster 1 — predicate DSL: Token, TokenWindow, TokenPredicate, AbstractPatternPredicate, AndPredicate, OrPredicate, NotPredicate, LemmaPredicate, TagPredicate, PosGroupPredicate, WordPredicate (11 files).
Cluster 2 — query execution core: QueryExecutor, BlackLabQueryExecutor, BlackLabSnippetParser, QueryResults (4 files).
Cluster 3 — semantic exploration: SemanticFieldExplorer (1 file).
Cluster 4 — grammar verification using predicate DSL: CQLVerifier (1 file, imports grammar.CQLParser).
issues.suggestion: Stage the reorg as follows: (1) Create query/predicate/ and move Token, TokenWindow, TokenPredicate, AbstractPatternPredicate, And/Or/NotPredicate, Lemma/Tag/PosGroup/WordPredicate there. Run: find src -name '*.java' | xargs grep -l 'import.*query\..*Predicate\|import.*query\.Token' to identify import updates needed. (2) Move CQLVerifier to grammar/ since it imports CQLParser and uses it to build predicates. (3) Optionally move SemanticFieldExplorer to a new exploration/ package if it grows. (4) Validate with: mvn test -q after each step.

issues.summary: CQLVerifier has strong import affinity with grammar/ but lives in query/, creating a cross-package placement mismatch
evidence: CQLVerifier imports and uses CQLParser (from grammar/) to parse patterns and then builds predicate trees.
It belongs to the grammar verification concern, not query execution.
From the directory_profiles, CQLVerifier is in query/ alongside BlackLabQueryExecutor — a semantically distant neighbor.
issues.suggestion: Move CQLVerifier to grammar/ (alongside CQLParser, CQLPattern, CQLParseException). Update import in test: src/test/java/.../query/CQLVerifierTest.java. Validate: mvn test -q -Dtest=CQLVerifierTest.

issues.summary: AbstractPatternPredicate.test() recompiles the regex pattern on every invocation instead of caching at construction time
evidence: In test(): String regex = wildcardToRegex(effectivePattern); return Pattern.matches(regex, value.toLowerCase());
Pattern.matches() compiles a new Pattern object on every call.
The pattern string is fixed at construction time (passed to the constructor, stored in final field pattern), so the compiled Pattern could be computed once in the constructor.
issues.suggestion: In the constructor, compute effectivePattern (applying isPatternCaseInsensitive()) and compile it: this.compiledPattern = Pattern.compile(wildcardToRegex(effectivePattern)). In test(), replace Pattern.matches(regex, ...) with compiledPattern.matcher(value.toLowerCase()).matches(). This eliminates per-call compilation for all predicate subclasses.

issues.summary: WordSketchApiServer mixes HTTP routing, parameter parsing, and JSON response construction in one 1042-line class
evidence: Each of the 11 handler methods begins with params = HttpApiUtils.parseQueryParams(...) and ends with a manual JSONObject/HashMap construction loop before sendJsonResponse — routing and serialization are not separated.

Five handlers (handleFullSketch, handleRelationQuery, handleFullDependencySketch, handleDependencyRelationQuery) contain structurally identical code: for (var result : results) { JSONObject word = new JSONObject(); word.put("lemma", ...); word.put("frequency", ...); word.put("logDice", ...); word.put("pos", ...); collocations.add(word); }
issues.suggestion: Extract a CollocationResponseBuilder helper that turns List<WordSketchResult> into JSONArray. Extract a RequestParams wrapper around HttpApiUtils.parseQueryParams to standardise parameter access. Each handler method should only call the executor and delegate serialization.

issues.summary: handleSemanticFieldExploreMulti embeds collocate-intersection algorithm directly in the API server handler
evidence: Lines 648-664 in WordSketchApiServer: the for-loop accumulating seedToCollocates and computing commonCollocates via retainAll is business logic (multi-seed intersection algorithm) that duplicates exploration intent from SemanticFieldExplorer.
SemanticFieldExplorer already has an exploreByPattern method for single-seed; multi-seed intersection is a natural extension of that API.
issues.suggestion: Add a SemanticFieldExplorer.exploreMultiSeed(Set<String> seeds, ...) method that returns a structured result. Move the intersection logic there. The API handler becomes a thin delegator.

issues.summary: SemanticFieldExplorer hosts five result data classes that belong with QueryResults
evidence: ExplorationResult, DiscoveredNoun, CoreAdjective, ComparisonResult, AdjectiveProfile, and Edge (6 inner classes) are defined inside SemanticFieldExplorer.java, inflating it to 690 lines.
QueryResults.java already houses WordSketchResult and ConcordanceResult — the existing pattern for shared result DTOs.
issues.suggestion: Move ExplorationResult, DiscoveredNoun, CoreAdjective, ComparisonResult, AdjectiveProfile, and Edge into QueryResults.java as static inner classes. SemanticFieldExplorer then only contains algorithm logic.

issues.summary: GrammarConfigLoader.RelationConfig record has accumulated 8+ methods covering pattern substitution, POS extraction, and deprel derivation
evidence: RelationConfig record defines getFullPattern, substituteHeadword, mergeLemmaConstraint, extractXposPattern, extractPosPattern, extractConstraintAttribute, collocatePosGroup, extractLabelContent, posGroupFromConstraint, getDeprel — effectively a service object inside a record.
substituteHeadword and mergeLemmaConstraint are pattern-manipulation logic that could live alongside PatternSubstitution.java in the api package.
issues.suggestion: Extract pattern-substitution logic (substituteHeadword, mergeLemmaConstraint, extractXposPattern) into PatternSubstitution or a new RelationPatternHelper class. RelationConfig should be a pure data record.

issues.summary: BlackLabQueryExecutor duplicates BlackLabSnippetParser extraction logic in private helpers
evidence: extractDependentLemmaFromGroup (lines 404-422) iterates LEMMA_ATTR matcher on group identity — identical to BlackLabSnippetParser.extractCollocateFromSnippet.
extractPosFromGroup (lines 429-446) iterates XPOS_ATTR then UPOS_ATTR — identical to BlackLabSnippetParser.extractPosFromMatch.
issues.suggestion: Delete extractDependentLemmaFromGroup and extractPosFromGroup from BlackLabQueryExecutor; call BlackLabSnippetParser.extractCollocateFromSnippet and BlackLabSnippetParser.extractPosFromMatch directly.

issues.summary: BlackLabSnippetParser.extractCollocateFromPattern always returns 'unknown' despite documenting that it extracts the collocate lemma
evidence: Lines 164-170: the method computes pos2Index = findLabelPosition(bcqlPattern, 2), checks whether pos2Index <= 0, but both branches unconditionally return 'unknown'. The computed pos2Index is never used.
Javadoc says 'Extract collocate lemma from the BCQL pattern for a given hit index' — the implementation violates this contract completely.
issues.suggestion: Either implement the method properly (use pos2Index and hitIndex to extract the collocate from matching hits), or remove it and its callers entirely if it is dead. If unimplemented intentionally, mark it @Deprecated and throw UnsupportedOperationException with a clear comment.

issues.summary: BlackLabQueryExecutor.getCorpusSize always returns 0L with no documentation that it is a stub
evidence: Lines 532-534: public long getCorpusSize() throws IOException { return 0L; }
No @deprecated tag, no TODO comment, no UnsupportedOperationException. Any caller relying on corpus size data silently receives zero.
issues.suggestion: Implement using blackLabIndex.metadata().tokenCount() or equivalent BlackLab API call, or mark the method as not-implemented with a clear // TODO comment and a log warning if called.

issues.summary: QueryResults.ConcordanceResult has five constructors that produce objects with different null fields, making it impossible to know which getters are safe to call
evidence: Constructor (String snippet, int start, int end, String docId) sets lemma, tag, word, rawXml, collocateLemma all to null.
Constructor (String sentence, String rawXml, int start, int end, String docId, String collocateLemma, long frequency, double logDice) sets lemma, tag, word to null.
Constructor (String sentence, String lemma, String tag, String word, int startOffset, int endOffset, String docId) sets rawXml, collocateLemma to null.
There is no type-level distinction between a 'full concordance result' and a 'grouped collocate result' — callers must know the construction path to safely call getters.
issues.suggestion: Split ConcordanceResult into two distinct classes: ConcordanceResult (sentence, start, end, docId, optional rawXml) and CollocateResult (collocateLemma, frequency, logDice, sentence). This makes the contract explicit at the type level.

issues.summary: CQLVerifier.verify and CQLVerifier.verifyForCollocate have identical implementations, creating a false contract distinction
evidence: verify (lines 77-95) and verifyForCollocate (lines 101-118) both call buildSteps(elements) then matchSequence(window, headwordPosition, steps, 0, new HashMap<>(), 0) with the same arguments and return the same VerificationResult.
The only documented distinction is 'looking for collocates' vs general verification, but the implementations are byte-for-byte identical.
issues.suggestion: Delete verifyForCollocate and replace all call sites with verify. If distinct semantics are needed in the future, implement them as an actual difference.

issues.summary: BlackLabSnippetParser.extractCollocateFromPattern computes pos2Index but never uses it, making both branches dead
evidence: Line 165: int pos2Index = findLabelPosition(bcqlPattern, 2);
Line 166-167: if (pos2Index <= 0) { return 'unknown'; }
Line 168-169: return 'unknown'; // this branch is only reached when pos2Index > 0, yet still returns 'unknown'
pos2Index is computed but not passed to any extraction call — the conditional is structurally dead.
issues.suggestion: If the method is intended to extract by position, use pos2Index to index into the pattern hit. If it is permanently unimplemented, mark it clearly or remove it.

issues.summary: CQLVerifier.matchSequence recomputes findInRange identically on each iteration of the repetition loop
evidence: Lines 224-253: for (int rep = step.minRep; rep <= step.maxRep; rep++) { List<Token> candidates = window.findInRange(headwordPosition, effectiveMinDist, effectiveMaxDist); ... }
findInRange arguments (headwordPosition, effectiveMinDist, effectiveMaxDist) are all invariant with respect to rep. The list is rebuilt from scratch on every loop iteration.
issues.suggestion: Hoist the findInRange call above the rep loop: List<Token> candidates = window.findInRange(...); for (int rep = step.minRep; rep <= step.maxRep; rep++) { for (Token candidate : candidates) { ... } }

issues.summary: SimpleTagger.determineVerbForm returns VBD (past tense) for 'am', which is a present-tense form
evidence: Line 354: if (word.equals("am")) return "VBD";
'am' is the first-person present tense of 'be'. The Penn Treebank tag for present-tense non-3rd-person verb is VBP, not VBD (which is past tense).
AUX_VERBS set includes 'am' so tagWord dispatches to determineVerbForm for 'am' after none of the suffix patterns match.
issues.suggestion: Change line 354 to: if (word.equals("am")) return "VBP"; — or add 'am' directly to VERB_FORMS with tag 'VBP' (consistent with how 'is' maps to VBZ there).

issues.summary: handleBcqlQueryPost declares boolean raw but never reads it
evidence: Line 984: boolean raw = obj.getBooleanValue("raw"); // Add raw output option
The variable 'raw' is never referenced again in the method body — no conditional on it, no passing to executor.
issues.suggestion: Either implement the raw-output toggle (if the feature is intended), or remove the declaration and the comment.

issues.summary: handleSemanticFieldExploreMulti has an unreachable null guard for commonCollocates after seeds.size() >= 2 is already enforced
evidence: Line 611: if (seeds.size() < 2) { sendError(...); return; } — guarantees at least 2 seeds.
Lines 648-663: for (String seed : seeds) { ... if (commonCollocates == null) { commonCollocates = seedCollocates; } else { commonCollocates.retainAll(...) } }
Lines 666-668: if (commonCollocates == null) { commonCollocates = new HashSet<>(); } — this null guard can only be reached if seeds is empty, which is already excluded.
issues.suggestion: Initialize commonCollocates before the loop using the first seed: Set<String> commonCollocates = new HashSet<>(collocatesFor(seeds.iterator().next())); then retainAll in subsequent iterations. Remove the post-loop null guard.

issues.summary: relationType is a raw String compared against magic literals 'SURFACE' and 'DEP' throughout the codebase
evidence: GrammarConfigLoader.RelationConfig record: String relationType field.
WordSketchApiServer.handleFullSketch, handleFullDependencySketch, and /api/relations handlers all compare with "SURFACE".equals(rel.relationType()) and "DEP".equals(rel.relationType()).
A typo in any comparison ('SURFACES', 'Dep') would compile silently and filter incorrectly.
issues.suggestion: Define a RelationType enum with SURFACE and DEP values. Change RelationConfig.relationType to RelationType (non-nullable). Replace all string comparisons with enum equality.

issues.summary: RelationConfig.explorationEnabled is boxed Boolean (nullable) with no documented null semantics
evidence: Record definition: Boolean explorationEnabled — boxed type means null is a valid value.
toJson() guards with 'if (explorationEnabled != null)', but no other code documents what null means semantically (disabled? not configured?).
Callers who test explorationEnabled == true will silently skip entries where the JSON had no 'exploration_enabled' field.
issues.suggestion: Change to primitive boolean with a documented default. Either read it with relObj.getBooleanValue("exploration_enabled", false) and store as boolean, or use Optional<Boolean> to make optionality explicit in the type.

issues.summary: POS group values are raw string literals scattered across multiple classes with no shared constant or enum
evidence: GrammarConfigLoader.posGroupFromConstraint returns 'adj', 'verb', 'noun', 'adv', 'prep', 'part', 'other' as String literals.
WordSketchApiServer.handleSemanticFieldExplore and handleSemanticFieldExploreMulti compare collocatePosGroup().equals("adj") to choose simplePattern.
SimpleTagger.TaggedToken.getPosGroup returns 'noun', 'verb', 'adj', 'adv', 'det', 'pron', 'prep', 'conj', 'punct', 'other'.
The sets do not align (e.g., SimpleTagger has 'det'/'pron'/'conj'/'punct' but GrammarConfigLoader does not).
issues.suggestion: Define a PosGroup enum (or shared constants class) with all valid values. Use it in GrammarConfigLoader, SimpleTagger, SemanticFieldExplorer, and the API server. This also surfaces the vocabulary divergence between the two POS-group schemas.

issues.summary: SemanticFieldExplorer result classes and field names hard-code 'Adjective'/'Noun' even though the explorer is BCQL-pattern-driven and can work with any collocate type
evidence: Inner classes: AdjectiveProfile, CoreAdjective, DiscoveredNoun.
ExplorationResult fields: seedAdjectives (Map<String,Double>), coreAdjectives (List<CoreAdjective>).
DiscoveredNoun field: sharedAdjectives (Map<String,Double>).

exploreByPattern accepts a bcqlPattern parameter and a relationName, and the grammar config already supports verb-object and other non-adjective relations — but all result naming implies adjectival modification only.
issues.suggestion: Rename: AdjectiveProfile → CollocateProfile, CoreAdjective → CoreCollocate, DiscoveredNoun → DiscoveredWord, seedAdjectives → seedCollocates, sharedAdjectives → sharedCollocates, coreAdjectives → coreCollocates. This removes the false assumption and aligns the API with the grammar-config-driven approach.

issues.summary: BlackLabSnippetParser.extractCollocateFromPattern is misnamed — it always returns 'unknown' and extracts nothing
evidence: Method named 'extractCollocateFromPattern' with Javadoc 'Extract collocate lemma from the BCQL pattern for a given hit index'.
Both code paths return the string literal 'unknown' — the name is a complete misnomer.
issues.suggestion: Fix the implementation to actually extract (see contract_coherence issue extract_collocate_from_pattern_stub), then the name will match. If removing the method, also remove all call sites.

issues.summary: WordSketchApiServer.normalizeRelationId is vaguely named — it maps user-facing alias strings to internal relation IDs
evidence: Method body (lines 1032-1039) is a switch mapping 'adj_modifier'/'modifier' → 'noun_modifiers', 'adj_predicate'/'predicate' → 'noun_adj_predicates', etc.
'normalize' suggests formatting/casing changes; this is alias resolution with semantic mapping to internal IDs.
issues.suggestion: Rename to resolveRelationAlias or mapRelationIdAlias to communicate what the method actually does.

issues.summary: CQLVerifier.verifyForCollocate implies different behavior from verify, but both methods are identical
evidence: verifyForCollocate Javadoc: 'Verify a CQL pattern against a token window, looking for collocates. The headword is at position 0 (conceptually).'
Both verify and verifyForCollocate call buildSteps and matchSequence with identical arguments and produce identical results.
issues.suggestion: Remove verifyForCollocate (see contract_coherence issue verify_and_verify_for_collocate_identical). One well-named method is clearer than two with artificial naming distinctions.

issues.summary: BlackLabSnippetParser name implies XML snippet parsing but the class also handles plain-text extraction and BCQL pattern inspection
evidence: extractPlainTextFromXml, detokenize, extractCollocateFromPlainText, extractCollocateFromMatchText — plain-text operations, not snippet parsing.
findLabelPosition, extractHeadword — BCQL pattern inspection, operating on query strings not XML snippets.
extractCollocateFromXmlByPosition, extractLemmaFromMatch — genuine XML snippet parsing.
issues.suggestion: Split the class: keep BlackLabSnippetParser for the XML-parsing methods; extract CqlPatternInspector (or inline into BlackLabQueryExecutor) for findLabelPosition/extractHeadword; move text utilities into a TextUtils class.

issues.summary: getTotalFrequency silently swallows all exceptions, hiding index access failures
evidence: catch (Exception e) { return 0L; } — no logging, no rethrow; callers receive 0 and silently produce logDice=0.0 for all collocates when the index is degraded
issues.suggestion: Add at minimum logger.warn('Failed to get frequency for lemma {}: {}', lemma, e.getMessage()); before return 0L, or rethrow as IOException if frequency lookup failure should be a hard error.

issues.summary: parseQueryParams silently discards URL decode errors with a comment-only catch block
evidence: catch (Exception e) { // Skip invalid parameters } — no logging; malformed percent-encoding in a parameter silently drops it, causing confusing downstream 'missing parameter' errors
issues.suggestion: Log the exception at DEBUG level: logger.debug('Skipping malformed query parameter: {}', pair, e); This preserves the skip behaviour while making problems diagnosable.

issues.summary: Bare Integer.parseInt / Double.parseDouble on user query params throws uncaught NumberFormatException as a 500
evidence: int topCollocates = Integer.parseInt(params.getOrDefault("top", "15")); — repeated across handleSemanticFieldExplore, handleSemanticFieldExploreMulti, handleConcordanceExamples, handleSemanticField without try-catch
A request with top=abc causes NumberFormatException to bubble up to the outer catch(Exception e) handler which returns HTTP 500 instead of 400
issues.suggestion: Extract a helper parseIntParam(params, key, defaultValue) that catches NumberFormatException and either returns the default or calls sendError(exchange, 400, 'Invalid integer parameter: ' + key). Apply consistently to all numeric param parsing.

issues.summary: handleFullSketch and handleFullDependencySketch log relation failures at DEBUG, masking production errors
evidence: catch (Exception e) { logger.debug("Relation {} failed for lemma {}", rel.id(), lemma, e); } — using DEBUG means a broken relation in production leaves no visible trace in default logging configuration
issues.suggestion: Promote to logger.warn() for unexpected failures so that relation-level errors surface in default log output. Reserve DEBUG for expected misses (e.g., no results).

issues.summary: BlackLabSnippetParser static Pattern fields are package-private instead of private
evidence: static final java.util.regex.Pattern LEMMA_ATTR = ...; — no access modifier, so package-private by default
All other utility-class constants in the codebase (LogDiceCalculator, LongIntHashMap) use private static final
issues.suggestion: Add private modifier to all Pattern fields in BlackLabSnippetParser (LEMMA_ATTR, LEMMA_ATTR_ANY, XPOS_ATTR, UPOS_ATTR, SENT_BOUND_LEFT, SENT_BOUND_RIGHT, XML_SENT_OPEN, XML_SENT_CLOSE). If BlackLabQueryExecutor uses them directly, expose them via package-private accessor methods instead.

issues.summary: WordSketchApiServer uses inline fully-qualified com.sun.net.httpserver types instead of imports
evidence: private com.sun.net.httpserver.HttpServer server; and private void handleFullSketch(com.sun.net.httpserver.HttpExchange exchange, ...) — the type is referenced by FQN in 10+ method signatures rather than imported
All other external types in the same file use standard imports at the top
issues.suggestion: Add import com.sun.net.httpserver.HttpServer; and import com.sun.net.httpserver.HttpExchange; at the top of WordSketchApiServer.java and replace all inline FQN usages.

issues.summary: BlackLabQueryExecutor exposes public methods not declared in QueryExecutor interface
evidence: findDependencyCollocations() and executeDependencyPattern() are public on BlackLabQueryExecutor but absent from QueryExecutor
API server casts directly to BlackLabQueryExecutor to call executeDependencyPattern() or relies on the wider type, defeating interface abstraction
issues.suggestion: Either promote these methods to the QueryExecutor interface (with a default empty implementation or documented as optional capability) or keep them package-private and add a DependencyCapableExecutor sub-interface that BlackLabQueryExecutor implements.

issues.summary: WordSketchApiServer (1042 LOC, 10+ endpoints) has zero tests
evidence: No test class named *ApiServer* or *ApiTest* exists in src/test/
The server handles all user-facing functionality including input parsing, error responses, and response shape — all untested
issues.suggestion: Add WordSketchApiServerTest using a mock QueryExecutor (already supported by the constructor) and an embedded HttpServer instance. Test: (1) 400 on missing required params, (2) 400 on malformed numeric params, (3) response shape for /api/sketch/{lemma}, (4) 500 on executor IOException.

issues.summary: SemanticFieldExplorer has no tests despite having a mock-friendly constructor
evidence: SemanticFieldExplorer(QueryExecutor executor) constructor is available and used by the API server — trivially testable with a mock
The explore and exploreByPattern methods contain non-trivial aggregation logic (intersection of collocate sets, minShared filtering) that is high-value to test
issues.suggestion: Add SemanticFieldExplorerTest with a stub QueryExecutor that returns controlled collocation lists. Test: (1) intersection logic, (2) minShared=1 returns all collocates, (3) empty seeds list, (4) single-seed returns non-empty result.

issues.summary: Integration tests guard on a hardcoded Windows path that never exists in CI
evidence: private static final String INDEX_PATH = "D:\\corpora_philsci\\bi"; — Windows absolute path in all three files
Tests skip via assumeTrue(indexDir.exists()) so they never fail but also never run in any automated build
issues.suggestion: Read the index path from a system property or environment variable: String.getProperty('test.index.path', null). Document in README that setting this property enables integration tests. This allows local runs with a real index while keeping CI green without skipping.

issues.summary: Sibling sketch endpoints return different top-level keys for equivalent data
evidence: /api/sketch/{lemma} returns { lemma, patterns: { relId: { collocations: [...] } } }
/api/sketch/{lemma}/{relation} returns { lemma, relation, collocations: [...] } — flat, no wrapping
/api/sketch/{lemma}/dep returns { lemma, type, relations: { relId: { collocations: [...] } } } — uses 'relations' not 'patterns'
Clients cannot write a unified parser for the same conceptual resource across these three endpoints
issues.suggestion: Standardize on a single envelope: { lemma, relation_type, relations: [ { id, name, collocations: [ { lemma, logDice, frequency } ] } ] }. The single-relation endpoint becomes a filtered view of the same shape with one element in 'relations'.

issues.summary: GET endpoints do not validate HTTP method; POST/PATCH requests are silently processed
evidence: POST /api/bcql and POST /api/visual/radial both check exchange.getRequestMethod() and return 405 if not POST
All GET endpoints (/api/sketch, /api/relations, /api/semantic-field/explore, etc.) have no method check — a POST to these endpoints will execute the handler normally
issues.suggestion: Add a shared guard in HttpApiUtils: static boolean requireMethod(HttpExchange exchange, String method) throws IOException — returns false and sends 405 if the method doesn't match. Call at the top of all GET handlers.

issues.summary: explore and explore-multi handlers use different pattern-substitution paths for the same config object
evidence: handleSemanticFieldExplore: bcqlPattern = relationConfig.get().getFullPattern(seed) — head already substituted
handleSemanticFieldExploreMulti: bcqlPattern = relationConfig.get().pattern() — raw pattern, head NOT substituted, substitution deferred to executeSurfacePattern
This produces different effective queries for the same relation, making results incomparable across the two endpoints
issues.suggestion: Unify: both handlers should use the same codepath. Since explore-multi loops over seeds, move head substitution into the loop: String bcqlPattern = relationConfig.get().getFullPattern(seed); before calling executeSurfacePattern. Remove the raw pattern reference in explore-multi.

issues.summary: CORS headers set inconsistently — some handlers use HttpApiUtils, others inline directly on exchange
evidence: HttpApiUtils.sendJsonResponse() sets Access-Control-Allow-Origin: * centrally (lines 24, 39)
WordSketchApiServer handlers at lines 913-915 and 950, 968-970 also set CORS headers directly on exchange, duplicating and potentially shadowing the utility method
No auth check or rate limit on any endpoint — acceptable for a local research tool, but CORS policy is not applied uniformly
issues.suggestion: Centralise all CORS header logic in HttpApiUtils. Remove the inline Access-Control-Allow-Origin assignments from WordSketchApiServer handlers so all responses go through one code path.
